title: Playable single-player game with themed UI and persistent leaderboard

requirements.summary: Add a playable in-browser single-player 2D game with clear rules, score, win/lose states, and keyboard + touch controls.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can start a new game from the UI without reloading the page.
The game supports keyboard controls on desktop and touch controls on mobile.
The game includes a visible score and an end-of-game state with a restart option.
All user-facing text is in English.

file_operations.description: Define shared 2D game types/constants (game phases, directions, timing, scoring) to keep gameplay logic consistent and testable.
Implement the core 2D game loop as a React hook (new game, pause/resume if needed, score tracking, win/lose detection, restart) with a single source of truth for state transitions.

Create on-screen touch controls (direction/action buttons) suitable for mobile and usable on desktop; wire control events into the game hook. Use shadcn-ui primitives (e.g., Button/Card) for consistent controls styling; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Render the gameplay HUD (score, state indicators, restart) and ensure all user-facing text is English. Use shadcn-ui components for layout/styling consistency; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Compose the full game experience (menu -> gameplay -> game over) including keyboard handlers for desktop and integration of the on-screen controls for mobile; ensure restart starts a new game without full page reload.
Create the App shell that renders the new GameScreen as the primary experience (or as a top-level section) and ensures it is reachable from the initial render (no orphan screens).
Ensure the application entry continues to render the App shell that includes the game experience (no navigation dead-ends) and remains SPA-friendly (no reload-based starts).

requirements.summary: Add signed-in principal high-score persistence with best-score saving and a global leaderboard, while keeping gameplay available when signed out.
acceptanceCriteria: If the user is signed in, finishing a game can submit the score to the backend.
The backend stores and returns each principal’s best score (only updates when the new score is higher).
The frontend can display the current user’s best score and a global top leaderboard list.
If the user is not signed in, the UI clearly indicates that saving scores requires sign-in (English text).

file_operations.description: Create React Query hooks to fetch the current user's best score (when authenticated), submit a finished-game score, and fetch the global leaderboard using the existing actor from useActor.

Add a Login/Logout button using Internet Identity via the authorization component guidance (useInternetIdentity + query cache clearing on logout). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Display the signed-in user's best score and a global top leaderboard list; if signed out, show an English message that sign-in is required to save scores. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card/Table/Button) for the leaderboard UI; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

On game completion, if authenticated, submit the final score and refresh best-score/leaderboard; if not authenticated, render an English callout explaining sign-in is required to save scores and provide the LoginButton.
Wire the leaderboard UI into the game screens (menu/game over/leaderboard view) so users can always access the global leaderboard and see their best score when signed in.

requirements.summary: Apply a consistent non-blue/purple visual theme across all game screens with responsive, readable UI styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-related screens share a consistent visual style (background, typography, button styles, spacing).
The primary palette does not rely on blue/purple as the main theme colors.
UI remains readable and usable on small screens.

file_operations.description: Add a CSS entry point to match the existing import in frontend/src/main.tsx and centralize game theme tokens/utilities; ensure the primary palette uses non-blue/purple colors (e.g., warm neutrals + green/orange accents) and supports small-screen readability.

Update global CSS variables and base styling to match the new coherent game theme (avoid blue/purple as primary), keeping contrast accessible and typography consistent across menu, HUD, game over, and leaderboard.
Apply consistent layout, spacing, and theme-aware classes across menu/gameplay/game-over/leaderboard sections so the game UI feels cohesive and remains usable on small screens.
Adjust/extend Tailwind theme tokens if needed to support the chosen non-blue/purple palette and consistent component styling across all game screens.
